children.heading: .—Federal Agencies, Mixed-Ownership Government Corporations, and the District of Columbia
children.label: a .—Federal Agencies, Mixed-Ownership Government Corporations, and the District of Columbia

children.text: a federal agency;
a mixed-ownership Government corporation (as defined in ); orsection 9101 of title 31
the District of Columbia.

children.label: i
children.text: a qualified nonprofit agency for the blind (as defined in ); orsection 8501(7) of title 41

children.label: ii
children.text: a qualified nonprofit agency for other severely disabled (as defined in ); andsection 8501(6) of title 41

children.label: B
children.text: providing a commodity or service to the Federal Government under chapter 85 of title 41.

children.heading: .—Use of services
children.label: 2 .—Use of services
children.text: A nonprofit agency receiving services under this subsection shall use the services directly in making or providing to the Government a commodity or service that has been determined by the Committee for Purchase From People Who Are Blind or Severely Disabled under to be suitable for procurement by the Government.section 8503 of title 41

children.heading: Use of Certain Supply Schedules.—
children.label: c Use of Certain Supply Schedules.—
A
children.text: Automated data processing equipment (including firmware), software, supplies, support equipment, and services (as contained in Federal supply classification code group 70).

children.label: B
children.text: Alarm and signal systems, facility management systems, firefighting and rescue equipment, law enforcement and security equipment, marine craft and related equipment, special purpose clothing, and related services (as contained in Federal supply classification code group 84 or any amended or subsequent version of that Federal supply classification group).

children.heading: .—Voluntary use
children.label: 2 .—Voluntary use
children.text: In any case of the use by a State or local government of a Federal supply schedule pursuant to paragraph (1), participation by a firm that sells to the Federal Government through the supply schedule shall be voluntary with respect to a sale to the State or local government through such supply schedule.

children.label: A
children.text: The term “State or local government” includes any State, local, regional, or tribal government, or any instrumentality thereof (including any local educational agency or institution of higher education).

children.label: i
children.text: the governing body of any Indian tribe, band, nation, or other organized group or community located in the continental United States (excluding the State of Alaska) that is recognized as eligible for the special programs and services provided by the United States to Indians because of their status as Indians, and

children.label: ii
children.text: any Alaska Native regional or village corporation established pursuant to the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act ( et seq.).43 U.S.C. 1601

children.label: C
children.text: The term “local educational agency” has the meaning given that term in section 7013 of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965.

children.label: D
children.text: The term “institution of higher education” has the meaning given that term in section 101(a) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 ().20 U.S.C. 1001(a)

children.heading: Use of Supply Schedules for Certain Goods and Services.—
children.label: d Use of Supply Schedules for Certain Goods and Services.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Administrator may provide for the use by State or local governments of Federal supply schedules of the General Services Administration for goods or services that are to be used to facilitate recovery from a major disaster declared by the President under the Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act ( et seq.), to facilitate disaster preparedness or response, or to facilitate recovery from terrorism or nuclear, biological, chemical, or radiological attack.42 U.S.C. 5121

children.heading: .—Determination by secretary of homeland security
children.label: 2 .—Determination by secretary of homeland security
children.text: The Secretary of Homeland Security shall determine which goods and services qualify as goods and services described in paragraph (1) before the Administrator provides for the use of the Federal supply schedule relating to such goods and services.

children.heading: .—Voluntary use
children.label: 3 .—Voluntary use
children.text: In the case of the use by a State or local government of a Federal supply schedule pursuant to paragraph (1), participation by a firm that sells to the Federal Government through the supply schedule shall be voluntary with respect to a sale to the State or local government through such supply schedule.

children.heading: .—Definitions
children.label: 4 .—Definitions
children.text: The definitions in subsection (c)(3) shall apply for purposes of this subsection.

children.heading: Use of Supply Schedules by the Red Cross and Other Qualified Organizations.—
children.label: e Use of Supply Schedules by the Red Cross and Other Qualified Organizations.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Administrator may provide for the use by the American National Red Cross and other qualified organizations of Federal supply schedules. Purchases under this authority by the American National Red Cross shall be used in furtherance of the purposes of the American National Red Cross set forth in , United States Code. Purchases under this authority by other qualified organizations shall be used in furtherance of purposes determined to be appropriate to facilitate emergency preparedness and disaster relief and set forth in guidance by the Administrator of General Services, in consultation with the Administrator of the Federal Emergency Management Agency.section 300102 of title 36

children.heading: .—Limitation
children.label: 2 .—Limitation
children.text: The authority under this subsection may not be used to purchase supplies for resale.

children.heading: .—Qualified organization
children.label: 3 .—Qualified organization
children.text: In this subsection, the term “qualified organization” means a relief or disaster assistance organization as described in section 309 of the Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act ().42 U.S.C. 5152

children.heading: .—Duty of Users Regarding Use of Supply Schedules
children.label: f .—Duty of Users Regarding Use of Supply Schedules
children.text: All users of Federal supply schedules, including non-Federal users, shall use the schedules in accordance with the ordering guidance provided by the Administrator of General Services.
